senses.gloss: waistband, the strap at the upper part of trousers to secure their attachment

examples.text: ولا نظير لتككهم ومحفوريَّاتهم وقرمزهم وأنماطهم وصبغهم وفاكهة تسمَّى الزُّوقال وقسبويه وسمك يقال له الطِرِّيخ ولهم تين وشاه بلُّوط في غاية الجودة.
examples.english: There is nothing resembling their waistbands, their streaked rugs, their crimson, their druggets, their dyes, and the fruit called cornel, and their dentexes, and their fish called pearl mullet. And they have figs and chestnuts of finest quality.

examples.text:
لَيْتَهُ جَادَ بِأَدْنَى قُبْلَةٍ
مِنْهُ إِذْ ظَنَّ بِمَا تَحْتُ ٱلتِّكَكِ
examples.english:
If only he were more generous with nearer a kiss
compared to that when he judged with what was under his belt!

etymology.text:
Borrowed from Aramaic תיכתא.

Instance noun from تَكَّ (takka).
